questionHtml: Explain how the information in the box labelled B indicates that the gene is not sex-linked.
markschemeHtml:
1. if it was sex-linked it would be on the x chromosome;
  2. there cannot be a heterozygous male if the trait is sex-linked
  3. males would pass the allele to their daughter;
  4. daughter is not shown as heterozygous so it is not sex-linked;

meta.title: Explain how the information in the box labelled B indicates that the gene is not sex-linked.
breadcrumbLabels: D3.2.12. Haemophilia as an example of a sex-linked genetic disorder
